title: generate-crud configuration
description:
Controls which tables and fields are included or excluded by the generate:crud script (scripts/generate-crud.ts).

Place this file at the root of the app directory and reference it via $schema so VS Code provides IntelliSense.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference — used by editors for IntelliSense. Set to a relative path pointing to this file.

exclude.description:
Drizzle table variable names to skip entirely.

No Zod schema, no routes, and no controllers are generated for these tables. Use this for security-sensitive or internal tables where even a schema definition should not be auto-generated (e.g. FGA configuration, internal key stores).

schemaOnly.description:
Drizzle table variable names for which only the Zod schema is generated — no Express routes or controllers.

Useful for documenting a table's shape in OpenAPI (via generate-openapi.ts) without exposing public CRUD endpoints. Typical candidates: audit logs, hard-delete logs, and other append-only or system-managed tables.

tables.description:
Per-table field-level exclusions.

Keys are Drizzle table variable names (camelCase, matching the export name in the schema file). Each entry controls which columns are excluded from the generated body validation schema and/or from SELECT responses.

excludeFromBody.description:
Column names to remove from BodySchema (POST) and UpdateSchema (PATCH).

Use this for server-managed fields that clients must never supply directly:
- Credential fields: password, salt, gaKey
- Token fields: refreshToken, smsOtpPin
- Security state: revoked, retryCount, retryLimit, retryReset, smsVerified, smsLastSent
- Any column whose value must only be set by business logic, not by user input

excludeFromResponse.description:
Column names to omit from SELECT in generated controllers.

When this list is non-empty the generator produces an explicit column list instead of SELECT * so the listed columns are never returned in API responses. Use this to prevent sensitive credentials or secrets from leaking through list/detail endpoints.

Note: you should also exclude these fields from the body (excludeFromBody) to prevent clients from setting them on write.
